# Cosmic Signature — Full Documentation

> Cosmic Signature is a procedural on-chain art protocol on Arbitrum. Participants make gestures during a Performance Cycle; every gesture shapes the cycle's final Signature. When the cycle finalizes, the protocol distributes its reserves across multiple allocation tracks — including Protocol Guild, the funding mechanism for 170+ Ethereum core contributors.

## About Cosmic Signature

Cosmic Signature combines generative on-chain art with coordinated community participation and public-goods support. It runs on Arbitrum, a Layer 2 scaling solution for Ethereum, to offer fast, low-cost participation while inheriting Ethereum-level security.

The NFT artwork is derived from the three-body problem in physics — simulating the motion of three planets in space and tracing their trajectories, producing unique, reproducible visuals. Every NFT is imprinted with a seed stored in the smart contract, and the image and video are produced from that seed using an open-source Rust program.

## Website Structure

### Main Pages

- **Home** (https://www.cosmicsignature.com/): Live gesture interface showing the active cycle, Cycle Reserve, countdown timer, recent gestures, and gesture submission form
- **Gallery** (https://www.cosmicsignature.com/gallery): Browse and search all imprinted COSMIC NFTs with filtering and pagination
- **How It Works** (https://www.cosmicsignature.com/how-it-works): Step-by-step guide covering protocol mechanics, gesture submission, allocation tracks, and tips
- **FAQ** (https://www.cosmicsignature.com/faq): Questions organized into categories covering getting started, allocations, cycle mechanics, tokens, technical details, and Cosmic Council coordination
- **Statistics** (https://www.cosmicsignature.com/statistics): Protocol analytics including cycle history, gesture volumes, allocation distribution, and participant stats
- **Anchoring** (https://www.cosmicsignature.com/anchoring): Anchor COSMIC NFTs (CST) to receive ongoing Anchor Distributions from each Cycle Reserve
- **Allocations** (https://www.cosmicsignature.com/allocation): Current and historical Cycle Reserve details
- **Contracts** (https://www.cosmicsignature.com/contracts): Smart contract addresses, ABIs, and fund distribution breakdown
- **Outreach** (https://www.cosmicsignature.com/marketing): Outreach Allocation program for community contributors
- **Imprint** (https://www.cosmicsignature.com/imprint): Interface for imprinting new tokens
- **Current Cycle** (https://www.cosmicsignature.com/current-cycle): Detailed view of the active cycle including gesture history, leaderboards, contributions, and allocation distribution

### User Pages

- **My Tokens** (https://www.cosmicsignature.com/my-tokens): View your owned COSMIC NFTs
- **My Anchors** (https://www.cosmicsignature.com/my-anchors): Manage your anchored tokens and view Anchor Distributions
- **My Allocations** (https://www.cosmicsignature.com/my-allocations): Track your allocations across cycles
- **My Statistics** (https://www.cosmicsignature.com/my-statistics): Personal participation statistics

### Data Pages

- **Recipient History** (https://www.cosmicsignature.com/recipient-history): Complete history of all cycle recipients
- **Named NFTs** (https://www.cosmicsignature.com/named-nfts): NFTs that have been given custom names
- **Attached NFTs** (https://www.cosmicsignature.com/attached-nfts): History of NFTs attached to gestures
- **ETH Contributions** (https://www.cosmicsignature.com/eth-contribution): History of ETH contributions
- **Allocation Finalized** (https://www.cosmicsignature.com/allocation-finalized): Record of finalized allocations
- **Public Goods Contributions** (https://www.cosmicsignature.com/public-goods-contributions-cg): Public Goods Allocation records
- **Public Goods Retrievals** (https://www.cosmicsignature.com/public-goods-retrievals): Retrieval records from the Public Goods Vault

## Protocol Mechanics — Detailed

### Gesture Process

1. Each cycle opens with a Calibration Window where the Gesture Cost descends from the Calibration Ceiling toward the Calibration Floor over roughly two days
2. Every new gesture increases the Gesture Cost by approximately 1% and extends the Cycle Finalization Time by about an hour
3. The participant who makes the Final Gesture when the Cycle Finalization Time expires may finalize the cycle and receive the Signature Allocation
4. Gestures can be made with ETH or Cosmic Signature Tokens (CST, ERC-20)
5. CST gestures use a Calibration Window — the CST Gesture Cost drifts downward continuously within its own window
6. Random Walk NFT holders can attach their token to a gesture for a 50% reduction in Gesture Cost (once per wallet)

### Allocation Distribution Per Cycle

- **25%** — Signature Allocation (participant who made the Final Gesture)
- **19%** — Anchor Distributions (distributed to all anchor-holders)
- **10%** — Public Goods Allocation (beneficiary coordinated by the Cosmic Council)
- **6%** — Stellar Selection allocations (four randomly selected participants share this reserve)
- **Remaining** — Carries forward into the next Cycle Reserve

### Per-Gesture Imprints

- 100 CST (Cosmic Signature Tokens) imprinted per gesture
- One Stellar Selection entry for end-of-cycle selections
- Eligibility to be an Allocation Recipient for a COSMIC NFT

### NFTs Imprinted Per Cycle

12 COSMIC NFTs are imprinted each cycle:
- 1 for the Signature Allocation Recipient
- 11 for Stellar Selection recipients (5 gesture-based + 4 RandomWalk anchor-holder + 2 additional)

### Special Titles

- **Endurance Champion**: The participant who remained the Last Participant for the longest consecutive period
- **Chrono Warrior**: The participant who held the Endurance Champion title for the longest consecutive period

### Time Mechanics

- Time added per gesture starts at 1 hour and gradually increases over time (~10-20% per year)
- This slows cycle-duration growth and limits total NFT supply, maintaining scarcity

## Tokens

### CST (Cosmic Signature Token)

- Type: ERC-20
- Imprinted: 100 CST per gesture made
- Uses: Alternative gesture currency (Calibration Window), Cosmic Council coordination signalling
- Tradeable on Uniswap and other Arbitrum DEXes

### COSMIC NFT

- Type: ERC-721
- Art: Generative, based on three-body problem physics simulation
- Storage: Seed stored on-chain; images reproducible via open-source Rust program
- Supply: Limited by the increasing time-per-gesture mechanism
- Tradeable on OpenSea and other Arbitrum NFT marketplaces

### RandomWalkNFT

- Partner NFT project
- Holders receive a 50% Gesture Cost reduction in Cosmic Signature (one-time per wallet)
- Can be anchored to the protocol for additional Stellar Selection entries

## Technical Architecture

- **Blockchain**: Arbitrum (Ethereum Layer 2 rollup)
- **Smart Contracts**: Solidity, immutable after ownership renunciation
- **Frontend**: Next.js 16, React 19, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS
- **Wallet Integration**: RainbowKit, wagmi, supporting MetaMask, Coinbase Wallet, WalletConnect
- **Data**: TanStack React Query for real-time data fetching
- **Monitoring**: Sentry for error tracking, Google Analytics for usage

## Coordination & Trust

- The team can adjust protocol parameters only during cycle activation windows
- Once stable, ownership will be permanently renounced via renounceOwnership()
- After renunciation, protocol rules become immutable
- All smart contracts are open-source and verifiable
- The team does not receive funds directly — all reserves flow through the immutable smart contract
- If the team disappears, the protocol is self-sustaining (seeds on-chain, code open-source)

## Community & Support

- **Twitter/X**: https://x.com/CosmicSignature
- **Discord**: https://discord.gg/bGnPn96Qwt
- **Website**: https://www.cosmicsignature.com

## Frequently Asked Questions

### Getting Started

Q: What is Cosmic Signature?
A: Cosmic Signature is a procedural on-chain art protocol on Arbitrum. Participants make gestures during a Performance Cycle; every gesture shapes the cycle's final Signature. When the cycle finalizes, the protocol distributes its reserves across multiple allocation tracks — including Protocol Guild.

Q: How does a Performance Cycle work?
A: Each cycle opens with a Calibration Window where Gesture Cost descends from the Calibration Ceiling toward the Calibration Floor over roughly two days. The first gesture starts the Cycle Finalization Time (initially ~24 hours). Each subsequent gesture increases the Gesture Cost by approximately 1% and extends the Cycle Finalization Time by about an hour. When the Cycle Finalization Time expires, the participant who made the Final Gesture may finalize the cycle and receive the Signature Allocation.

Q: What types of gestures are available?
A: Gestures can be made with ETH or Cosmic Signature Tokens (ERC-20 CST). You may also attach a Random Walk NFT to your gesture for a 50% reduction in Gesture Cost. The CST Gesture Cost drifts downward continuously within its own Calibration Window.

Q: Can I participate if I don't own any NFTs?
A: Yes, anyone can participate by making a gesture. You do not need to own an NFT. Holding a Random Walk NFT grants a 50% reduction in Gesture Cost.

### Allocations

Q: What is the Signature Allocation?
A: The Signature Allocation includes a unique COSMIC NFT, 25% of the Cycle Reserve (ETH), and any attached tokens (ERC-20/ERC-721) from other projects.

Q: What do I receive for each gesture?
A: Each gesture yields three things: 100 CST (Cosmic Signature Tokens), one Stellar Selection entry for end-of-cycle selections, and eligibility to be an Allocation Recipient for a COSMIC NFT.

Q: How does Stellar Selection work?
A: Each gesture also acts as a Stellar Selection entry. At the end of each cycle, the smart contract selects four entries to share 6% of the Cycle Reserve. Additionally, five participants receive a COSMIC NFT, and four RandomWalk anchor-holders also receive an NFT.

Q: How does anchoring work?
A: After receiving Cosmic Signature Tokens from Stellar Selections, you can anchor them and continue receiving Anchor Distributions. Anchoring distributes 19% of the protocol contract balance each cycle, shared equally among all anchored-token owners.

### Cycle Mechanics

Q: How does the Gesture Cost increase with each gesture?
A: Each gesture increases the current Gesture Cost by 1%. This exponential growth adds a strategic element. At the start of a new cycle, the Gesture Cost resets to approximately 100× lower than the Final Gesture Cost of the previous cycle.

Q: What is CST-based gesturing?
A: When you gesture with ETH, you are imprinted with 100 CST. These tokens can be used to make another gesture through the Calibration Window, where the CST Gesture Cost decreases over time if no gestures are made.

Q: What is an Endurance Champion?
A: The participant who remained the Last Participant for the longest consecutive period of time.

Q: What is a Chrono Warrior?
A: The participant who held the Endurance Champion title for the longest consecutive period of time.

### Tokens & NFTs

Q: What makes COSMIC NFTs unique?
A: Each NFT is imprinted with a seed stored in the smart contract. The image and video are produced from this seed using an open-source Rust program based on the three-body problem in physics.

Q: How are the NFT images created?
A: The images are generated from the three-body problem in physics, simulating the motion of three planets in space and tracing their trajectories.

Q: Is the NFT supply limited?
A: Yes, due to the gradual increase in time added per gesture, the pace of new NFT creation slows over time, effectively limiting the total supply.

### Arbitrum & Technical

Q: What is Arbitrum and why is Cosmic Signature deployed on it?
A: Arbitrum is a Layer 2 scaling solution for Ethereum that speeds up transactions and reduces fees. Cosmic Signature is deployed on Arbitrum to provide a better participant experience.

Q: Can I use my existing Ethereum wallet on Arbitrum?
A: Yes, the same private keys are valid on both networks. You just need to adjust your wallet's network settings to connect to Arbitrum.

### Trust & Coordination

Q: What controls does the team have?
A: The team can adjust certain protocol parameters only during cycle activation windows. Once the project is stable, ownership will be permanently renounced.

Q: Does the team receive funds directly?
A: The team does not receive funds directly. All reserves flow through the immutable smart contract and are allocated according to the protocol's rules.

Q: What happens if the team disappears?
A: The protocol is self-sustaining. Since NFT seeds are stored on-chain, anyone can regenerate the images using the open-source Rust program.
